postName: Senior Backend Engineer – Acme Data Corporation 2025
shortDescription: Design and build the systems that ingest, store, and index job postings and resumes at scale.
longDescription:
As a Senior Backend Engineer at Acme Data Corporation, you will own the data layer that everything else depends on. Your systems will ingest job postings and resumes from multiple sources, normalize and store them, and make them searchable and retrievable at low latency.

This is a systems-focused role. You will make meaningful decisions about database schemas, indexing strategies, cloud infrastructure, and API design. You will work closely with the ML team to ensure that the data they need is available in the right shape, and with the product team to expose that data through reliable APIs.

We run on cloud infrastructure (AWS or GCP) and value services that are simple to operate, easy to reason about, and built to handle failure gracefully.
jobBenefits:
- Competitive salary and equity
- Fully remote with flexible hours
- Health, dental, and vision insurance
- $2,500 annual learning and conference budget
- Home office stipend

estimatedSalary.comment: Base salary; total comp includes equity

values: Design and operate ingestion pipelines that pull job postings and resumes from external sources and normalize them into a consistent internal schema
Own the database layer — schema design, query optimization, migrations, and ongoing performance tuning
Build and maintain full-text and vector search indexes that power candidate and job discovery
Expose clean, versioned APIs consumed by the product frontend and ML pipelines
Ensure services are observable, with structured logging, metrics, and on-call runbooks
Collaborate with the ML team to deliver precomputed features and raw data in formats their pipelines require
5+ years of backend engineering experience building and operating production services
Strong command of at least one relational database — schema design, indexing, and query optimization in PostgreSQL or equivalent
Hands-on experience deploying and running services on AWS or GCP
Working knowledge of full-text or vector search systems such as Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, or pgvector
Solid API design instincts — RESTful or RPC, versioning, error handling, and backwards compatibility
Experience writing testable, well-documented code and participating in rigorous code review
Experience ingesting and normalizing data from heterogeneous external sources or third-party APIs
Familiarity with event streaming systems such as Kafka or Google Pub/Sub
Prior work on search-heavy or data-intensive products
Exposure to ML feature engineering or data pipeline workflows
Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools such as Terraform or Pulumi
